title: Input Injection Defense
concept: Filtering untrusted environment inputs (like scraped text) to prevent prompt injection hijacking the agent.

exercises.prompt: What do we need to secure our AI agents from harmful inputs?
exercises.explain: Input filtering is crucial for protecting AI agents from dangerous or misleading information.

cards.title: What is Input Injection Defense?
cards.body: Input Injection Defense helps our AI agents stay safe by filtering out harmful or untrusted information before it can cause problems.

cards.title: Defining Input Injection
cards.body: Input Injection occurs when untrusted information is fed into an AI system, potentially leading to undesired operations.

cards.title: Think of a Security Guard
cards.body: Imagine a security guard checks everyone before they enter a building. Input filtering acts like this guard for AI, blocking harmful input.

cards.title: Example of Unsafe Input
cards.body: An example of unsafe input might be text that tricks an AI into performing unauthorized actions.

cards.title: Myth: All Input is Safe
cards.body: Some people think all information is useful. In reality, untrusted inputs can harm AI agents.

cards.title: Input Filtering vs. Unsafe Input
cards.body: Visualize how filtering works: the guard keeps out the bad while letting the good in.

left.text: Malicious commands that trick the AI.

right.text: Clean, safe information for the AI.

exercises.prompt: What does input filtering help protect against?
exercises.explain: Input filtering specifically protects AI against harmful inputs that could manipulate or disrupt its operations.

exercises.prompt: Input filtering is unnecessary for trustworthy environments.
exercises.explain: False. Input filtering is essential for safeguarding AI, even in environments thought to be safe.

exercises.prompt: Input filtering protects against harmful or _____ inputs.
exercises.sentence: Input filtering protects against harmful or untrusted inputs.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'untrusted inputs', as these are the types of inputs filtering is meant to protect against.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an advantage of input filtering?

options.text: An AI that works without errors

exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'Reduced risk of harmful disruptions' because input filtering minimizes the chance of the AI being affected by unsafe data.

exercises.prompt: How does input filtering enhance AI reliability?

options.text: By blocking harmful and untrusted inputs
By adding more features to the AI
By reducing the need for updates

exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'By blocking harmful and untrusted inputs.' Input filtering ensures only safe data interacts with the AI, maintaining its reliability.
